original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded referral fee allows 100% LP fee extraction or DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In `GaugeFactoryCL.sol`, `setReferralFee` allows the admin to set `dibsPercentage` to any value without an upper bound check. In `GaugeCL._claimFees`, the fee sent to the dibs address is calculated as `claimed0 * referralFee / 1000`. If the admin sets `dibsPercentage` to 1000, 100% of LP fees are diverted from voters to the foundation. If set > 1000, the subtraction `claimed0 -= _dibsFeeToken0` will underflow, reverting the transaction and permanently blocking all fee distribution.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Lack of bounds on fee parameters allows a compromised or malicious admin to silently steal all yield or brick the protocol's fee distribution mechanism.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function setReferralFee(uint256 _dibsFee) external onlyAllowed { dibsPercentage = _dibsFee; } // No require(_dibsFee <= 1000)

preconditions: Admin (or compromised key) calls setReferralFee with value >= 1000

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: claimed0 * referralFee / 1000 without bounds on referralFee
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Admin steals 100% of LP fees intended for voters

economic_questions: Is the admin a multisig or an EOA?
Can the fee be changed back to restore functionality if set > 1000?
validation_requirements: Test setting fee to 1000 and verify 100% extraction
Test setting fee to 1001 and verify underflow DoS

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: 100% of all CL pool trading fees

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Destroys voter incentives for CL pools

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All trading fees generated by CL pools

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
